caveats: a: Automation potential at separately classified administrators, carriers, or portfolio managers is not evidence for this industry under the frozen lens.
rho: Adoption evidence from insurance administrators or portfolio managers would require an explicit cross-industry bridge and would not cure the lens mismatch.
e: This is a taxonomy-based structural zero for the stated lens, not a claim that insurance-fund ecosystems have no service providers.
s5: Fund or plan persistence is not equivalent to operating-company survival.
q: Economics of third-party administrators and carriers belong to other NAICS industries.
d5: Persistent demand for insurance protection does not establish demand for an in-scope service firm in this code.
o: Governance of a sponsor-controlled fund is not a proxy for acquiring and operating an independent service business.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: The classified entity is a fund or plan serving its sponsor, firm, employees, or members and earns property income rather than revenue from selling services. Any external fee revenue belongs to a differently classified carrier, administrator, or portfolio manager.
lens.excluded_activities: The defined captive or member-serving insurance-fund vehicles are excluded because they do not sell outsourced services to external customers. Fee- or contract-based insurance carriers, third-party insurance administrators, and portfolio managers are also excluded because Census classifies them in NAICS 5241, 524292, and 523920, respectively.
lens.included_activities: Potentially included only if an establishment correctly classified in NAICS 525190 supplies recurring outsourced services to unrelated external customers. The official definition identifies no such activity: this industry consists of legal entities organized to provide insurance exclusively for a sponsor, firm, employees, or members.

caveats: Automation potential at separately classified administrators, carriers, or portfolio managers is not evidence for this industry under the frozen lens.
a.change_evidence: A verified census of correctly classified 525190 establishments that sell recurring services to unrelated customers, with role-level payroll and workflow data, would establish whether a qualifying population exists and permit estimation.
a.rationale: AI-addressable labor share is undefined for the frozen lens because the official industry definition contains captive insurance-fund vehicles rather than qualifying external-service firms, and the supplied dataset has no compensation-to-receipts anchor.

caveats: Persistent demand for insurance protection does not establish demand for an in-scope service firm in this code.
d5.change_evidence: A validated population of correctly classified external-service firms plus customer renewal, budget, and substitution data would support estimation.
d5.rationale: Five-year demand durability for a recurring outsourced-service offering is undefined because no such offering is part of the official 525190 activity set.

caveats: This is a taxonomy-based structural zero for the stated lens, not a claim that insurance-fund ecosystems have no service providers.
e.change_evidence: A revised official classification definition or establishment-level evidence that a correctly classified 525190 entity sells recurring services to unrelated external customers would challenge the structural zero.
e.rationale: The Census definition is a structural boundary: 525190 entities provide insurance exclusively for a sponsor, firm, employees, or members, while fee-based carriers, third-party administrators, and portfolio managers are assigned to other industries. Consequently, the share of this code's establishments eligible under the external-customer service lens is zero.

l.detail: SUSB scope excludes this activity, no IPS labor-compensation/output series at any level, and no other receipts source downloaded; labor_share left null (recorded gap).
l.rationale: dataset labor_share has no defensible value

n.detail: margin 0.2102 (margins.json[52]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Financial Svcs. (Non-bank & Insurance) EBITDA/Sales 21.02%. Note: Damodaran reports 0.00% for banks (EBITDA not meaningful); bank codes inherit this non-bank default and are flagged ESTIMATE like all n_band values.); revenue/employee $605,324 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 525); DATA GAP: no SUSB size-class data for this code; n_band could not be computed — the research run must supply it with a shown chain, quality ESTIMATE
n.rationale: dataset n_band has no defensible value

caveats: Governance of a sponsor-controlled fund is not a proxy for acquiring and operating an independent service business.
o.change_evidence: Evidence on ownership, licensing, governance, customer concentration, and day-to-day operations from correctly classified external-service establishments would permit estimation.
o.rationale: Owner operability is undefined because the frozen lens identifies no qualifying service-company operating model within 525190.

caveats: Economics of third-party administrators and carriers belong to other NAICS industries.
q.change_evidence: Contract-level pricing, delivery-cost, and customer-retention evidence from correctly classified 525190 external-service providers would permit a value-capture estimate.
q.rationale: Gross-benefit retention cannot be mapped to the defined industry because its entities do not earn customer service revenue; property income and member or sponsor funding are not the fee economics required by this construct.

caveats: Adoption evidence from insurance administrators or portfolio managers would require an explicit cross-industry bridge and would not cure the lens mismatch.
rho.change_evidence: Documented deployments, implementation constraints, and adoption outcomes from correctly classified 525190 external-service establishments would make this primitive estimable.
rho.rationale: Realizable adoption is undefined because no qualifying outsourced-service workflow is identified within the official 525190 population.

caveats: Fund or plan persistence is not equivalent to operating-company survival.
s5.change_evidence: Evidence of qualifying correctly classified establishments, followed by longitudinal survival records for that population, would permit estimation.
s5.rationale: Five-year survival conditional on being an eligible firm is undefined because the eligible population is structurally empty under the frozen lens.

principal_driver: The official NAICS boundary cleanly separates captive insurance funds from fee-based operating service providers.
principal_weakness: No establishment-level business register was available to test for rare miscoded or hybrid operators, so the conclusion relies on the official industry definition rather than an empirical enumeration.

sources.supports: Official definitions for subsector 525 and industry 525190, including the absence of service-sale revenue and cross-references for fee-based carriers, third-party administrators, and portfolio managers.
sources.title: NAICS Sector 52: Finance and Insurance

sources.supports: Adjacent context on member-benefit entities, including membership, control, fiduciary governance, and benefit restrictions; it is not used to quantify a primitive.
sources.title: Exempt Organization Sample Questions: Voluntary Employee Beneficiary Associations

title: Other Insurance Funds
